game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **海姆达尔 Heimdallr** Players control hacker Jason across two distinct gameplay modes: puzzle-solving and parkour, aiming to uncover a 15-year-old murder case. Puzzle segments involve interacting with a simulated computer interface, tapping on elements to decrypt code, solve logic puzzles, or combine 2-3 items to reveal clues. Parkour segments feature 2D side-scrolling platforming where players use virtual on-screen buttons to run left/right, tap to jump, and swipe down to slide under obstacles, requiring precise timing to avoid hazards. Progression is linear through story chapters, with successful completion of a segment unlocking the next, while collecting hidden data fragments (e.g., 5 per level) or making specific dialogue choices influences one of over 10 distinct ending branches. Failure in parkour segments results in a checkpoint restart, and the game concludes upon reaching one of the multiple narrative conclusions based on player choices and collected evidence.
game_info.description: You are Jason, a hacker investigating a 15-year-old murder case. Navigate through alternating puzzle and parkour segments to uncover the truth. In puzzle mode, decrypt codes and combine items to reveal clues. In parkour mode, run, jump, and slide through dangerous environments. Collect data fragments to unlock different story endings. Complete all chapters to solve the mystery!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate menus, move left/right in parkour mode
Space: Jump in parkour mode, Select/Interact in puzzle mode
Z: Slide under obstacles in parkour mode
Shift: Sprint in parkour mode
Enter: Start game
Esc: Pause/Unpause
R: Restart (from game over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and interaction testing. This test validates that the player can move through puzzle and parkour segments, interact with objects, and transition between game modes correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The test will navigate through the first puzzle segment by selecting the correct items and decryption options. Then it will move through the parkour segment using basic movement controls without attempting advanced strategies.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test successfully completes the first chapter (puzzle + parkour) without errors. Player position updates correctly, items are collected, and the game transitions smoothly between segments. No game-breaking bugs occur.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition testing. This test implements an optimal strategy to complete all game segments, collect maximum data fragments, and reach one of the winning endings.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The test will solve all puzzles optimally by selecting correct combinations, collect all data fragments in parkour segments using precise timing, and make strategic dialogue choices to unlock the best ending path.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The test reaches GAME_OVER_WIN state by completing all chapters. All available data fragments are collected (5 per level minimum). The game displays a victory screen with the unlocked ending based on collected evidence.
